skill.name: swarm-planner
skill.description: Creates dependency-aware implementation plans optimized for parallel multi-agent execution.
skill.summary: Builds structured implementation plans with explicit task dependencies for parallel agent work.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution and weak-cryptography findings are false positives from Markdown backticks, example task IDs, empty arrays, and the word description. No malicious intent, prompt injection, executable payload, credential access, or exfiltration pattern was found in SKILL.md. The skill has low operational risk because it asks agents to fetch documentation and save a plan file in the current workspace.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static Ruby shell execution alerts point to Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and template syntax. SKILL.md contains instructions for planning, not Ruby code or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown documentation and plan examples. No executable Ruby file, command invocation, or user input interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: Text Tokens Misidentified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match ordinary prose and template labels such as description. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or cryptographic API usage appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are metadata, prose, or plan template labels. There is no cryptographic context or implementation to review.

low_findings.title: Plan File Write and Documentation Lookup
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE_LOW: The skill asks the agent to use documentation retrieval and save a Markdown plan in the current working directory. This is expected for the skill purpose, but users should understand it may use network-backed documentation tools and create a local file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions explicitly request documentation retrieval and a saved plan file. These behaviors are legitimate, scoped, and visible to the user.

content.value_statement: Large code changes are hard to divide across agents without dependency conflicts. This skill creates clear plans with task order, validation steps, and parallel execution groups.
actual_capabilities: Investigates a codebase before proposing implementation tasks.
Creates task plans with explicit depends_on relationships.
Separates work into parallel execution waves.
Defines validation steps for each planned task.
Requests clarification when architecture or scope is ambiguous.
Uses a subagent review step to check dependency gaps.
limitations: It creates plans only and does not implement the planned changes.
It depends on available tools for documentation lookup and subagent review.
Plan quality depends on accurate project context and user scope.
It may create a Markdown plan file in the current working directory.

use_cases.title: Split a Feature Across Agents
use_cases.description: Break a feature into ordered tasks that several agents can execute without blocking each other.

use_cases.title: Plan a Refactor Safely
use_cases.description: Map dependencies, validation checks, and execution waves before changing shared code.

use_cases.title: Prepare Agent Work Packages
use_cases.description: Create clear task descriptions, locations, and validation criteria for delegated coding work.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Change
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused implementation plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm-planner to create a dependency-aware plan for adding [feature] to this project. Ask questions if scope is unclear.

prompt_templates.title: Plan With Constraints
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request with boundaries and validation needs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm-planner to plan [change]. Respect these constraints: [constraints]. Include validation steps and parallel execution groups.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Agent Refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for dividing risky work across agents.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm-planner to inspect the codebase and create a multi-agent refactor plan for [area]. Identify dependencies, risks, and review checkpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Plan With Documentation Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request involving current external libraries or APIs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use swarm-planner for [project goal]. Fetch current documentation for relevant dependencies, then create a plan with task waves and validation criteria.

output_examples.input: Plan a checkout flow update that touches database schema, API routes, and tests.
output_examples.output: A Markdown plan with an overview, prerequisites, dependency graph, task list, parallel execution waves, testing strategy, and risk mitigations.

output_examples.input: Plan a shared service refactor for multiple agents.
output_examples.output: A task breakdown showing root tasks, dependent tasks, blocked work, validation checks, and which work can start in each wave.

output_examples.input: Plan a feature when architecture details are unclear.
output_examples.output: A short clarification request with recommended questions before the final plan is created.

best_practices: Provide the goal, affected systems, constraints, and expected validation before invoking the skill.
Let the skill inspect the repository before asking it to finalize task dependencies.
Review the generated plan before assigning tasks to agents.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you need immediate code implementation instead of planning.
Do not skip clarification when architecture, scope, or ownership is unclear.
Do not treat generated execution waves as final without human review for high-risk changes.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. It creates implementation plans and explicitly says not to implement the plan.

faq.question: Why does it use documentation lookup?
faq.answer: It asks for current library and API documentation so planned tasks use accurate interfaces.

faq.question: Does it support parallel agent work?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires each task to list dependencies and groups tasks into parallel execution waves.

faq.question: Can it ask clarification questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It directs the agent to stop and ask when scope, constraints, or architecture are unclear.

faq.question: What file does it create?
faq.answer: It asks the agent to save a Markdown plan named for the planning topic in the current working directory.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The static high-risk findings were false positives, and the remaining behaviors are visible planning actions.
